About Cbus
Created by workers, for workers, Cbus Super is one of Australia’s most successful Superannuation funds. For over four decades we’ve proudly represented those who help shape Australia, hard-working individuals who deserve to make the most of their retirement, no matter the industry. As an award-winning fund we’ve been recognised for the benefits provided to our members and our innovative investment approach.  All while staying committed to our member first ethos as a proud Industry Fund.

About the role
The technical analyst supports the performance team by maintaining and validating investment data, creating performance database and new performance data feed.  Other responsibilities include assisting in compiling and analysing performance results to feed reports and dashboards, creating new analytics and insights for investment team.

The role involves working closely with Data and Engineering team, Front office team, as well as external technology suppliers and data providers to deliver accurate, complete, well governed performance data and reporting across a wide range of investments.

This role will suit candidates with an eye for detail that possess hands-on technical experience with investment data processes and performance analysis calculations.  This covers the following requirements;

Experience with interpreting, manipulating and analysing data in relational databases using SQL to ensure data integrity, accuracy and completeness. Including interpreting existing SQL scripts, writing queries in SQL, writing scripts to draw out useful business insights including using PowerBI;
Experience working within Azure ecosystem, SQL databases, function Apps, Data Factory, Logic Apps, and Databricks
Knowledge and practical experience working with investment data at security level, i.e. holdings, transaction, corporate actions, T&Cs
Experience with third party Investment systems such as Ortec PEARL, FactSet, BarraOne, Datastream, or Bloomberg.

Overview

Alchemy Partners is an Australian-owned and led boutique consulting firm that partners with clients to improve business performance through better collaboration. We help clients navigate complex operating environments by bringing structure, clarity and practical insight to their most challenging problems.

We are seeking a Process Analyst / Operational Designer to support the operational design of a major government digital platform currently under development.  This role focuses on ensuring the operational environment surrounding the platform is well designed, scalable and ready to operate, including service processes, workforce capability and support models.

About the Role

Working within a multidisciplinary delivery team, you will design the operational processes and service model required to support a new digital platform.

Key responsibilities may include:

Designing operational processes for platform administration and internal service teams

Defining transition points between automated system processes and manual operational processes

Co-designing the support service model including service channels, escalation pathways and service levels

Applying ITSM principles to support models spanning portal, API and helpdesk environments

Developing workforce capability and training guidance, including role profiles, competencies and onboarding approaches

Building a resourcing model for operational support, including peak demand, surge capacity and hypercare support

Defining internal corporate service touchpoints required for operational readiness (HR, finance, continuity planning)

Supporting the development of an Operations Coordination and Planning Framework

About You

You will bring experience designing operational processes or service models in complex delivery environments.  You are comfortable working at the intersection of operations, technology and service delivery, translating operational needs into practical processes and service models.

You may bring experience in:

Process analysis or operational design in government or highly governed environments

Designing service models, operating models or support frameworks

Business process modelling and service blueprinting

Applying ITSM or service management practices

Supporting operational readiness for digital platforms or systems

You are pragmatic, collaborative and comfortable working closely with delivery teams and operational stakeholders.

Security & Location

This is a Canberra-based contract role

You must hold a minimum Baseline Security Clearance

What We Offer

Initial contract role with potential extensions

Competitive hourly rate

Opportunity to work on a complex, meaningful government program

A professional, collaborative consulting environment that values clarity, quality and adaptability


How to Apply 

Please submit your resume outlining your relevant experience and current security clearance status.

Ad Text:
Our client is seeking an experienced Data Program Manager to lead the delivery of a new enterprise data warehouse within a complex Insurance or Financial Services environment. This role will be responsible for driving the end-to-end program delivery, coordinating multiple workstreams across data engineering, architecture, analytics, and business teams to ensure successful implementation.

The ideal candidate will have strong experience delivering large-scale data platforms, cloud data warehouses, and data transformation programs, with the ability to manage stakeholders across business, technology, risk, and regulatory teams.

Responsibilities

Lead the end-to-end delivery of a new enterprise data warehouse program, ensuring alignment with business strategy and data governance frameworks.

Manage multiple project streams including data architecture, data engineering, integration, analytics, reporting, and data governance.

Define and manage program roadmap, delivery plans, budgets, and timelines.

Coordinate cross-functional teams including data engineers, architects, BI developers, analysts, and business stakeholders.

Work closely with enterprise architecture and data governance teams to ensure alignment with data standards, security, and regulatory requirements.

Oversee data migration, integration, and transformation activities from legacy systems into the new data warehouse environment.

Identify and manage program risks, issues, dependencies, and change impacts.

Drive stakeholder engagement and executive reporting, ensuring clear communication of progress, risks, and outcomes.

Ensure delivery supports data-driven decision making, reporting, regulatory compliance, and advanced analytics capabilities.

Facilitate Agile or hybrid delivery frameworks across program teams.

Requirements

Proven experience as a Program Manager delivering large-scale data or analytics programs.

Strong experience delivering data warehouse, data lake, or enterprise data platform implementations.

Experience within Insurance or Financial Services environments.

Strong understanding of data architecture, ETL/ELT pipelines, data governance, and analytics platforms.

Experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g" Databricks).

Demonstrated ability to manage complex multi-stream programs with cross-functional teams.

Strong stakeholder management experience across business leaders, technology teams, and vendors.

Experience delivering data transformation and modernisation programs.

Experience with Agile delivery methodologies (SAFe, Scrum, Hybrid).
Strong communication and influencing skills.

Able to manage ambiguity and drive outcomes in complex environments.

If you think you have the above skills and experiences, click the 'Apply' button or send your resume to alex.nguyen@talentinternational.com

Ad Text:
Employment Type: Continuing contract
Remuneration: Senior position- competitive total package
Location: Kensington, NSW (Hybrid work arrangement available)


About UNSW
UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. Yes, we’re a large organisation with a diverse and talented community; a community doing extraordinary things. Together, we are driven to be thoughtful, practical, and purposeful in all we do. Taking this combined approach is what makes our work matter. It is an exciting time to join UNSW with the launch of the new strategy, Progress For All.

This role is based in the Internal Audit Team who work to provide objective assurance and consulting activity designed to add value across key operational and strategic processes. The team offers high level support for the delivery of the Internal Audit Plan in line with UNSW’s Progress For All Strategy and key risks by managing and executing projects included in UNSW’s Internal Audit Plan.

 

Why your Role Matters
As Internal Audit Manager, you’ll work as a trusted partner and advisor to provide independent, objective assurance to senior management and develop high-quality deliverables (reports, dashboard, scope documents) designed to support UNSW’s internal control environment and improve the efficiency and effectiveness of operations.

Leading reviews and investigations across the University, you’ll be confident in developing the scope and approach of audit engagements as per stakeholder needs. With a strong background in risk and controls, your role works to highlight and address business trends and deliver on projects across UNSW’s Internal Audit Plan.

This role reports to the Director, Internal Audit and works with Senior Internal Audit Managers to ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of internal control processes over key strategic and business process risks at the University.

 

Skills & experience: We’re looking for a strong background in a risk and controls environment, with a minimum of 5 years of internal audit experience. You will hold:

 

A postgraduate internal audit, accounting or equivalent qualification (e.g. CA, CIA or equivalent).
Risk and controls experience within professional services and/or relevant commercial environment.
Big 4 and prior internal audit experience in the higher education sector is highly regarded.
Strong project management experience, analytical skills, and proactive attitude to independently lead and execute internal audit activities.
Business presence, confidence and composure to quickly develop professional rapport and credibility.
Effective leadership, interpersonal and communication skills, excellent ability to communicate complex information – both verbal and written.
Experience in interacting with business stakeholders and working as a key member of a team.
Experience in using data analytics to support internal audit reviews is desirable.


The above offers a summary of the position, for further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the position description found on Jobs@UNSW.

 

How to Apply: Submit your CV & cover letter detailing your interest and suitability for the job before Sunday 29 March by 11:55pm.
Please note: Sponsorship is not available for this role, valid Australian working rights are required on application.

 

UNSW Benefits and Culture: People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: 

Flexible Working Options (work from home, flexible hours etc) 
Additional 3 days leave during December festive period.
Career development opportunities 
Up to 50% discount on UNSW courses
Flexible 17% Superannuation contributions, additional leave-loading payments and salary sacrifice.
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

Job queries contact: Katherine Molloy, Talent Acquisition Consultant
E: katherine.molloy@unsw.edu.au  (applications to the address cannot be accepted, please apply online)

 

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

Victorian Managed Insurance Authority (VMIA) 

Job Title – Project Manager 
Work Type – 12-month Fixed Term Contract, Ongoing  
  
We are seeking an experienced Project Manager to join our Innovation and Projects team to lead the successful delivery of complex business and digital transformation initiatives. In this role, you will collaborate with stakeholders across the organisation, drive project outcomes using agile and hybrid methodologies, and contribute to building a confident and resilient Victoria. 
   
Key highlights 

Central Location – Work in Melbourne CBD within close proximity to Flinders Street.   
Salary Range – $167,754 - $186,393 (total remuneration package inclusive of super). 
Hybrid work environment – Enjoy a mix of office and flexible work arrangements, fostering connection and strengthening networks. The majority of your working week will be in our collaborative office environment.  
  
About the role 
At VMIA, our purpose is to build a confident and resilient Victoria. As the Victorian Government’s insurer and risk adviser, we protect the people, places, and projects that help Victorians thrive through world-leading harm prevention and recovery. 
We are seeking an experienced Project Manager to join our Innovation and Projects team. In this role, you will partner with business stakeholders to successfully deliver VMIA projects using the VMIA Project Management Framework, which incorporates waterfall, agile, and hybrid methodologies. 
You will lead and supervise end-to-end project management activities on behalf of Executive Sponsors and Business Leads, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within scope, and within budget. This role reports to the Program Manager and plays a key role in driving innovation, digital transformation, and organisational resilience. 
You will also contribute to VMIA’s mission by actively supporting risk management, identifying and escalating risks, following controls, and promoting accountability across project delivery. 
Key responsibilities 
End-to-End Project Leadership and Delivery 

Lead and oversee all project activities from planning through to completion. 
Ensure deliverables and dependencies are achieved within expected timeframes and approved budgets. 
Project Artefact and Procurement Management 

Create and manage project artefacts, proposals, documentation, and presentations. 
Support procurement processes including Request for Proposal (RFP) activities. 
Change Management and Issue Resolution 

Proactively manage changes to project objectives. 
Identify potential issues and develop contingency plans. 
Provide recommendations to resolve complex project challenges. 
Project Risk Identification and Mitigation 

Identify, manage, and mitigate project risks and issues to ensure successful delivery. 
Project Management Best Practices 

Promote best practices and tools for effective project execution. 
Facilitate agile ceremonies and workshops to drive purposeful outcomes. 
Framework Alignment and Governance 

Ensure project activities align with VMIA’s Project Management Framework and governance standards. 
Manage project governance, resource planning, budgets, stakeholder reporting, and knowledge management. 
Project Reporting and Insights 

Monitor and report on project progress, providing insights and identifying opportunities for improvement. 
Stakeholder Engagement and Team Leadership 

Build strong stakeholder relationships across teams. 
Lead project teams using agile methodologies and support higher levels of scrum maturity. 
Vendor Management 

Manage vendor relationships and report on vendor performance in line with contract management plans. 
Governance, Compliance and Risk 

Identify and escalate risks or issues and ensure compliance with policies and controls. 
Health, Safety and Wellbeing 

Maintain a safe and respectful workplace by following safety procedures and reporting hazards. 
People and Capability 

Participate in performance and development processes. 
Apply feedback to improve performance and contribute to a positive team culture. 
  
Who you work with  
In this role, you will collaborate closely with a range of internal and external stakeholders to support successful project delivery. Internally, you will work with the Innovation and Projects Team, Information Technology Team, Data and Analytics Team, People & Culture Team, Finance Team, RICS, and the Senior Leadership Team to coordinate project activities, align priorities, and drive outcomes. Externally, you will engage with vendors and suppliers to manage deliverables, support procurement activities, and ensure services are delivered in line with contractual agreements and project requirements. 
  
What we are looking for 
We are looking for a project management professional who can deliver complex projects while building strong stakeholder relationships and driving positive outcomes. 
Successful candidates will demonstrate: 

Strong accountability and results-driven project leadership 
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills 
Ability to navigate and lead through organisational change 
Strong digital literacy and confidence using project tools 
Data-driven decision-making and analytical thinking 
A collaborative mindset and commitment to continuous improvement 
The ability to build trusted relationships with internal and external stakeholders 
  
Qualifications 

Tertiary qualification (or equivalent) in Project Management. 
Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or Professional Scrum Master (PSM) certification preferred. 
Proven experience managing complex and multifaceted projects, particularly in business and digital transformation. 
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence and maintain effective working relationships. 
Demonstrated leadership capability, including coaching and mentoring project team members. 
Experience delivering projects using Waterfall, Agile, and Hybrid methodologies. 
Experience managing change management activities and supporting technology transformation initiatives. 
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to report insights that support decision-making. 
Proficiency with project management tools and platforms including Microsoft services suite and JIRA. 
Experience managing organisational change and driving stakeholder adoption. 
Experience delivering projects within a program environment (advantageous). 
  
Why join VMIA? 
At VMIA, we believe diversity drives innovation. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their unique perspectives. 
We offer: 

Flex work: Hybrid working to support your best performance. 
Parenting support: Generous parental leave (14 weeks paid parental leave after six months of service for primary carers and 2 weeks for secondary carers) and breastfeeding-friendly facilities. 
Learning & Development: Annual investment ($2,500) in your growth, with in-house training, and LinkedIn Learning access in addition. 
Wellbeing: Wellness sessions, Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and ergonomic support. 
Culture & Lifestyle: Iconic office location, volunteer leave and life-stage support.  
VMIA encourages applications from candidates of all backgrounds, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, individuals with disabilities, culturally diverse communities, and members of the LGBTQIA+ community. 
How to apply  
We invite you to submit your CV and a cover letter. Use these to introduce yourself, highlight your fit for the role, and share why you are excited about joining VMIA. For any questions about the role or application process, please contact us at talentacquisition@vmia.vic.gov.au. 
At VMIA, we are dedicated to fostering an inclusive workplace. We warmly welcome applications from people of all gender identities, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, individuals with disabilities, and those from culturally diverse backgrounds.  
If you have any accessibility needs during the recruitment process, please let us know at talentacquisition@vmia.vic.gov.au. 
Salary Information 
At VMIA, we offer competitive salaries based on the role's classification and your skills and experience. Typically, new employees start at the minimum of the salary range, however, consideration may be given to starting above the minimum of the band should you demonstrate extensive experience in a similar role or possess unique and desirable skills and capabilities required to perform the role.  
Other relevant information 
Before starting at VMIA, preferred candidates will complete pre-employment screenings. These assessments may include: 

Working Eligibility: Verification of your right to work in Australia. 
Employment History: Confirmation of your past employment details including employment dates and compensation.  
Academic Credentials: Verification of your educational qualifications to ensure they are accurate and legitimate. 
National Criminal History Check: A check for any criminal record, which may involve manual vetting by ACIC, potentially causing delays. 
Pre-employment Misconduct Check: Used to verify the identity, integrity, and credentials of potential employees and verifies no past misconduct. 
Financial Background Checks: Applicable for certain roles, such as Heads of Departments and Finance team members. 
We value a collaborative work environment, so employees are expected to spend the majority of your working week in the office. Candidates from interstate must be willing to relocate to Victoria.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:




Senior Data Engineer | Data & Analytics | Sydney, AUS

Work on cutting-edge enterprise data solutions using Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT, and Python.

Collaborate with a high-performing, agile team to deliver impactful insights across Deloitte.

Continuous professional growth through mentoring, training, and exposure to emerging data engineering technologies.

Deloitte Technology is seeking a skilled Senior Data Engineer to join our Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) team in Sydney. This is a permanent role where you'll play a key part in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable data pipelines and workflows that power Deloitte's data-driven initiatives. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with EDW pod leads and the Senior Data Engineering Manager, contributing to high-impact projects across the business.

What will your typical day look like?

Day to day you will:

Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

Transform raw data into actionable insights using Data Build Tools (DBT) and Python.

Build and maintain data integrations across systems using APIs.

Monitor and optimise workflows for performance and cost-efficiency.

Lead project delivery pods, mentor junior team members, and conduct code reviews.

Collaborate with data modelers, analysts, DBAs, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Document processes, workflows, and system architecture to maintain clarity and knowledge sharing.

About the team

Deloitte Technology's EDW team powers enterprise-wide data initiatives, enabling business users to make informed, data-driven decisions. You'll join a collaborative and high-performing team working in an agile environment, tackling complex data challenges and continuously improving the EDW platform.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, with expertise in Databricks, SQL, Python, Azure Data Factory, and DBT.

Experience designing, building, and optimising data pipelines and workflows.

Hands-on experience with API-based data integrations and version control systems (Azure DevOps, GitHub).

Strong understanding of data architecture patterns and cloud data storage solutions.

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and adaptability.

Collaborative mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Advanced degrees or relevant certifications in Azure or Databricks are a plus.

Desirable: Knowledge of Azure Fabric and emerging data engineering technologies.

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating, and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - up, into new areas, or across the globe.

We embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and cultivate inclusion, making our work environment a space where everyone belongs.

We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave, and a return-to-work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

What You Need to Apply for This Role:

Comprehensive experience in end-to-end finance processes and related financial activities.

Demonstrated ability to develop and implement systems and processes within a complex environment, including policy and practice development.

Ad Text:
Hybrid work environment
Full-time/Permanent
Play an important part shaping the future of our iconic Australian institution


The Manager, Financial Markets Risk will jointly lead the second-line oversight of financial markets risk across the Bank, ensuring that we identify, analyse and manage the risks related to our operations in financial markets, in a robust, fit-for-purpose fashion that meets the high internal governance and risk standards expected of a public institution. Given the fundamental impact the Bank's decisions have on the Australian economy, financial markets and financial institutions, this role is critical in safeguarding the integrity of the Bank's balance sheet and operations in financial markets and achieving the Bank's policy objectives.


This role is ideal for a financial risk professional with a strong ability to interpret quantitative analysis, and who brings excellent analytical, writing and stakeholder engagement skills. Experience in financial markets and a desire to apply risk skills, experiences and tools to a central bank's unique remit and operations are essential. Experience leading or contributing to second‑line challenge in a complex financial or policy environment is highly valued.


Key Responsibilities:


Provide independent second-line review and challenge of first-line risk identification, analysis and risk management around 'day to day' financial portfolio management (e.g. market risk, credit risk, liquidity risk, stress testing, capital adequacy).
Provide similar second-line challenge on the implications of significant changes in monetary and financial stability policies.
Monitor central bank, industry and regulatory developments and assess their impact on financial market risk practices.
Prepare and present financial market risk insights to senior management, risk committees, and the Board to support both policy and governance determinations.
Support the development and maintenance of the financial market risk management framework, policies, and procedures in line with relevant standards.
Mentor and lead staff and contribute to a culture of analytical excellence and continuous improvement.
Drive continuous enhancements to second‑line oversight capabilities, including strengthening risk insights, challenge processes and governance artefacts.


Experience


8+ years of experience in financial market risk within a financial institution or regulator, preferably in a second-line or oversight capacity.
Tertiary qualifications in a quantitative discipline (e.g. mathematics, statistics, finance, economics, actuarial science).
Strong understanding of financial risk management governance, analytical techniques and models (e.g. VaR, stress testing, IRRBB), and regulatory standards. The latter including familiarity with APRA Prudential Standards (e.g. CPS20, APS220, CPS110, APS116, APS117, APS210, CPG229).
Excellent analytical, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills.
Familiarity with financial market data, trading and risk systems, and with general quantitative tools (e.g. Excel); familiarity with R is advantageous.
Prior experience in a central bank, financial regulator or regulated financial institution, with flexibility to apply risk management principles to a central bank's policy remit.


Your Team


Reporting to the Senior Manager Financial, Market and Model Risk, within the Risk Management team (Line 2), you will play a leading role in establishing effective second-line review, challenge, and insight across the Bank's material risk classes. Establishing strategic and trusted relationships across the Bank is fundamental to the team's success. The Risk Management team supports the consistent and effective application of the Bank's framework for managing risk and compliance obligations, both at the enterprise level and for individual business units across the Bank. It also advises on and implements the risk management framework governing the Bank's operations in financial markets.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

The Role

In this position, you'll be responsible for the analysis and insight that underpins the entire change strategy. You'll partner with SMEs, functional consultants and the broader OCM team to:

Lead current‑state process discovery across Finance, Procurement, Maintenance and other SAP ECC areas.
Review, validate and refine Signavio process maps to reflect true business practices.
Identify variations, manual workarounds, spreadsheets, and custom processes hidden within the business.
Translate S/4HANA system and process changes into clear, structured business and role impacts.
Develop key OCM deliverables including change impact assessments, stakeholder impacts, and training inputs.
Support workshops, interviews and business engagement activities that feed into readiness and adoption planning.

This is a hands‑on, high‑visibility position that plays a critical role in ensuring the business is prepared for S/4HANA.

What We're Looking For

To be successful, you'll bring:

Strong background in process analysis, process mapping or business analysis.
Experience working with Signavio (highly desirable) or similar tools.
Solid understanding of SAP ECC and/or S/4HANA processes and transaction flows.
Experience working on large ERP or transformation programs.
Exposure to organisational change impact assessment.
Strong facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills.
Excellent documentation skills and an ability to translate complexity into clarity.

Industry experience in transport, utilities or asset‑intensive environments is a bonus but not essential.

Why You'll Love This Role
Long-term engagement
A major program with strong funding and clear direction
A mature, collaborative OCM/Solution Adoption team
The chance to do real process and change worknot surface‑level communications or admin
A role where your analysis directly shapes training, adoption and future ways of working

Boeing Defence Australia (BDA) is currently seeking an Reliability Analyst to join the P8 team.

Why this is exciting

Contribute to an enduring sovereign capability supporting maritime and national defence missions.
Success in this role improves fleet readiness and mission assurance. That visibility means your contributions are recognised and can drive long‑term improvements to national capability.

What you'll get to do

Support design and implementation of a Reliability Driven Readiness Framework to deliver and sustain FMC status for the RAAF P‑8A fleet.
Monitor capability health, identify long‑term reliability risks and develop mitigation strategies to protect fleet readiness.
Develop and execute reliability‑focused asset strategies and continuous improvement actions using MTBF, FMEA and condition‑based monitoring.
Contribute to BDA digital sustainment tool deployment to capture reliability metrics and enable predictive maintenance.
Track reliability KPIs (MTTR, reliability growth) and report FMC progress to senior leadership.
Shape and align reliability initiatives with broader program objectives as part of the Sustainment Program Leadership Team.

Who we're looking for

Dynamic, innovative and think outside the box.
Team oriented, influential and comfortable working in a matrix environment.
A clear communicator who engages and motivates diverse teams and stakeholders.
Outcome focused, with strong critical thinking and advanced problem‑solving skills.
Experienced in asset management principles, data analysis and reliability engineering techniques.
(Desirable) Logistics or technical Defence background with demonstrated people leadership.

Security requirement

Applicants must be Australian Citizens to meet defence security requirements.

Location

RAAF Base Edinburgh, Adelaide.  Hybrid work options available

Boeing values that you will live and lead by

Integrity - We are honest, transparent and accountable.
Safety - We always put safety first in design, manufacturing and operations.
Quality - We deliver products and processes that meet the highest standards.
Customer focus - We aim to understand and exceed our customers' mission needs.
Teamwork & One Boeing - We collaborate across teams and partners to accomplish shared goals.
Inclusion & Respect - We value diverse perspectives and create an environment where everyone can contribute.

Why join the P8 Australia team

Support a critical, long‑term maritime patrol capability that protects Australia's maritime interests.
Be part of a collaborative team delivering operational outcomes in partnership with the RAAF and industry.
Career growth and visibility — develop defence logistics, supply‑chain, financial modelling and stakeholder management skills that accelerate career progression and open future opportunities.

Include a current CV that includes your work history and qualifications.

The role:
At Teachers Health, we’re committed to delivering exceptional outcomes for our members through continuous improvement and innovation. As part of our strategic transformation agenda, we are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join the Strategic Transformation & Innovation team.

In this role, you will work across a cross-functional environment to capture, manage and translate business requirements into successful technology and business outcomes. You’ll act as the critical bridge between business stakeholders and delivery teams, ensuring the right solutions are defined, prioritised and delivered efficiently.

This is a highly collaborative role where you’ll partner with stakeholders across the business to define and document requirements, support solution delivery, and embed consistent BA practices that support both Agile and Waterfall delivery.

Attractive leave benefits

Hybrid working model (flex your work/life balance)

Subsidised private health insurance

Discounts on our products and services

Generous employee wellbeing program

Easily accessible by public transport

Teachers Health was created by teachers for teachers over 70 years ago and is the only health fund dedicated to the education community. As a not-for-profit health fund, we exist for our members, covering the lives of over 500,000 teachers, education staff, and their families.

Strong experience in business process analysis, workflow documentation and requirement gathering

Proficient in Jira, Confluence, Visio and Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

Strong stakeholder management, facilitation and communication skills

Ability to translate complex scenarios into simple, structured solutions

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capabilities

Experience with Agile/SCRUM, Waterfall or Hybrid delivery

Bonus points for:


Experience in insurance, health insurance or regulated environments

Understanding of the insurance value chain, products and systems

Knowledge of relevant Australian regulations impacting the insurance industry

Exposure to modern technology trends in insurance

Data Governance Manager

Sydney

$160-200k

Hyrbid

Position Overview

We are working with a leading financial services client who are looking for an experienced data governance professional to lead the development and implementation of enterprise-wide data governance and data quality practices.

This role sits within the enterprise analytics function and is responsible for strengthening data management capabilities, improving data quality, and embedding governance frameworks that enable effective decision-making and risk management.

The successful candidate will play a key role in establishing data ownership models, operationalising critical data elements, and improving visibility of data across the organisation through enhanced lineage and governance processes.

A core focus of the role will be partnering with business leaders and data stakeholders to improve accountability for data assets and drive a culture of effective data stewardship.

Key Responsibilities

Data Governance

Develop and implement enterprise data governance policies, standards and procedures

Establish governance structures to support effective management of organisational data

Facilitate governance forums and working groups to support cross-functional collaboration

Data Quality Management

Implement frameworks to monitor and improve data quality across critical datasets

Define and track key data quality metrics

Establish preventative and detective controls across the data lifecycle

Critical Data Elements

Identify and maintain a catalogue of critical data elements

Map controls and quality measures to key data assets

Ensure ongoing monitoring and governance of critical data

Data Lineage and Metadata

Develop enterprise data lineage for key datasets and reporting flows

Improve visibility of how data moves across systems and business processes

Maintain metadata and documentation supporting data governance activities

Data Risk Management

Support the development of a data risk management framework aligned with enterprise risk practices

Identify and assess data-related risks across business processes

Implement controls and monitoring mechanisms to mitigate risk

Data Ownership and Stewardship

Define and document responsibilities for data owners, stewards, and consumers

Embed accountability for data quality within business functions

Support collaboration across departments where shared data ownership exists

Data Literacy and Adoption

Promote greater understanding of data management practices across the organisation

Develop training and communication initiatives to support adoption of governance frameworks

Skills and Experience

Significant experience in data governance, enterprise data management, or data risk management

Demonstrated experience implementing data governance frameworks within complex organisations

Strong knowledge of data quality management, metadata management and data lineage practices

Experience working in regulated environments such as financial services, banking or insurance

Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive cross-functional initiatives

Experience with reporting and visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau

Familiarity with industry standards and frameworks such as DAMA-DMBOK

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Your new company

Join an organisation that thrives on innovation, collaboration and continuous improvement. You'll step into a dynamic environment where forward thinking leadership is valued, ideas are encouraged, and high quality service delivery is at the heart of every decision. This is a place where you can make a real impact, driving performance, shaping smarter financial practices, and empowering a team to achieve outstanding results.




Your new role

Step into a pivotal leadership role where you'll drive performance, empower a skilled team, and elevate financial operations across a complex organisation. This is your opportunity to lead with influence, shape smarter ways of working, and deliver high impact outcomes in a fast paced, service driven environment.


You will:

Lead the delivery of research and financial accounting services, including post award contract setup, variations, financial statements and milestone billing.

Primary Function:

This role ensures compliance with relevant accounting standards, regulatory requirements, and internal policies. The position plays a key role in financial governance, financial analysis, audit coordination, and continuous improvement of reporting processes.


Responsibilities include:

Ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards (e.g., IFRS / AASB / GAAP as applicable).

Prepare statutory financial statements and disclosures.

Maintain accuracy and integrity of financial data and reporting systems.

Regulatory and Compliance

Ensure compliance with financial regulations and reporting requirements.

Maintain up-to-date knowledge of accounting standards and regulatory changes.

Manage statutory reporting obligations including tax, FBT, regulatory filings, and corporate reporting requirements.

Develop and maintain financial reporting policies and procedures.

 

Audit Controls

Ensure strong internal financial controls and compliance with internal policies.

Financial Analysis and Insights

Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes.

Develop dashboards and reporting tools for management reporting.

Provide strategic insights to senior leadership based on financial data.

 

Team Leadership

Foster a culture of accuracy, accountability, and continuous improvement.

They have done this by constantly developing cutting edge models & constructs which in turn deliver predictive capability of future consumer behaviours.

There is a clear path for career progression for the right individual into the CEO position.

This role is about establishing and maintaining strong executive relationships with key clients whilst managing the external reputation and brand of the company through active involvement in the business community and industry groups where relevant.

This role is responsible for the financial performance of the division, a substantial & critical component of the role will ensure accurate pipeline management, forecasting and commercial activity to deliver upon business expectations.

The Individual
This role requires an individual that comes from the research & advisory industry, however, also has strong exposure to a specific customer base being financial services to drive deep meaningful commercial relationships. They will be a 'hunter' by nature with a sound track record of driving business growth within both existing customers but also bringing on new logos. They will also;

Have executive presence, they will be able to hold themselves competently and confidently across the executive suite, building credibility to deliver compelling outcomes for their clients.
Have a well-rounded background and career, with experience working in a more structured, process driven environment, paired with exposure to smaller more entrepreneurial organisation where they have developed or nurtured a natural growth mindset and want to continue expanding on this.
Be a proven leader, has experience leading high performing teams and has a natural skillset to take their team on a journey to deliver these results.
Be excited about the proposition of career progression, with a plan to take a step up into a CEO position in the near future

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Pricing Specialist to take ownership of end-to-end pricing activities. Reporting to the Senior Pricing Manager, you will operate with a high degree of autonomy, blending deep technical analysis with commercial strategy.

This is not just a back-room modelling role. You will also have the unique opportunity to liaise with the global Achmea international pricing community, with potential opportunities for international travel.

Key Responsibilities

You will manage regular pricing reviews and drive technical excellence across our portfolio. Your core focus areas will include:

End-to-End Pricing Management: overseeing portfolio profitability, incorporating technical modelling learnings, and accounting for claims inflation and regulatory changes.

Technical Modelling: Developing, maintaining, and refining technical models across relevant lines of business, ensuring alignment with Group protocols.

Commercial Impact: Analysing and applying commercial adjustments to optimise conversion and retention rates.

Strategic Insight: Monitoring the competitive landscape and external factors (such as regulatory changes like the Cyclone Reinsurance Pool, and macroeconomic trends) to inform pricing strategy.

Data & Innovation: Creating data sets to support pricing initiatives, monitor outcomes, and assisting in the design of new pricing algorithms.

Technical Stack: Strong proficiency in Excel and general insurance modelling tools (e.g., Emblem, Radar, Earnix). Strong experience using R and/or Python is essential.

Analytical Mindset: Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to spot trends, understand data limitations, and devise strategies to improve performance.

Communication Skills: The ability to articulate complex analytical results in a professional, easy-to-understand manner to non-technical stakeholders.

Study support and professional development opportunities.

About the Role

Wisr is scaling its core lending business while building new products that deepen customer value and improve unit economics. We are looking for a Head of Product who brings deep lending expertise, strong execution discipline, technical fluency, and strategic clarity.

This role exists to set product direction, strengthen governance and operating rhythm, and elevate product craft across squads, ensuring we consistently ship the highest-impact work.

You combine deep expertise in lending, product, and technology with commercial judgment and leadership maturity.

What you’ll do

The role is anchored around five core capability areas as detailed below -

Execution Excellence — Constant delivery outcomes

• You ensure disciplined, predictable portfolio execution without becoming a squad-level backlog owner.

Partner with Engineering, Design, Data, Operations, Growth and Risk  leaders to ensure squads have clear goals, priorities and executable plans.

• Guide trade-offs across squads and manage dependencies to maintain a reliable delivery cadence.

• Strengthen product governance: decision logs, outcome tracking, instrumentation and post-launch learning loops.

• Embed experimentation discipline (smoke tests, prototypes, controlled releases) with clear success metrics and decision points.

• Create transparency across the product portfolio so leadership understands progress, risk and return.

Lending Expertise — Depth of craft

• You understand the Australian lending landscape in depth and have a point of view on what creates differentiation.

• Deep understanding of the Australian lending market, competitors and structural trends.

• Strong understanding of pricing mechanics and lending economics — comfortable modelling business cases

• Actively engage in credit policy and risk meetings to improve product attractiveness without compromising credit integrity.

• Deep understanding of regulatory obligations in Australia, including responsible lending, DDO and CDR.

Immersed in underwriting and collections performance, using insights to improve product and portfolio outcomes.

Technology & Data Mindset — Technical fluency

• You are a technically and data-native product manager.

• Work closely with Engineering to define scalable, pragmatic technical approaches that deliver business outcomes at speed.

• Champion data-led decision-making across squads; thrive in product analytics and instrumentation.

• Ensure product decisions are grounded in measurable customer behaviour and portfolio performance.

• Stay close to emerging technologies (AI, Distributed Ledgers) that may unlock structural advantage.

• Translate complex technical constraints into clear commercial trade-offs.

Strategic Mindset — Set direction

• You provide clarity on what Wisr builds, why it matters, and how it creates durable advantage.

• Define and evolve a clear product vision and roadmap aligned to Wisr’s company strategy and innovation agenda.

• Articulate the future of Wisr’s lending and adjacent products — features, pricing, economics and competitive differentiation.

• Lead annual and quarterly product planning, translating strategy into roadmaps, OKRs and investment cases.

• Make explicit trade-offs across the portfolio to maximise long-term value creation.

• Partner with Risk, Finance and Legal to ensure product strategy aligns with regulatory and funding constraints.

People and Product Leadership — Raising the bar

• You build capability, not dependency.

• Lead, mentor and develop Product Managers, fostering customer obsession, learning and psychological safety.

• Set clear standards for product craft: problem framing, PRDs, experimentation, metrics and stakeholder management.

• Introduce pragmatic best practices that improve outcomes — not process for process’ sake.

• Foster strong cross-functional collaboration and shared accountability with Design, Engineering, Data and Delivery.

• Represent Product in executive forums with clarity, credibility and commercial depth.

What you’ll bring

• 10+ years of product management experience, with 5+ years leading Product Managers in fintech, SaaS or high-growth technology environments.

• Demonstrated success launching and scaling products (0→1 and 1→n).

• Strong understanding of lending economics, credit risk, technology platforms and regulated environments.

• Proven ability to define product vision and align cross-functional teams to measurable outcomes.

• Data-driven mindset with experience in analytics and experimentation.

• Systems thinker who moves comfortably between strategy and delivery detail.

• Exceptional communicator able to influence senior stakeholders.

• Experience with AI-enabled products (preferred but not essential).

What success looks like in your first 12 months:

• Cohesive product vision — A clearly articulated two-year roadmap aligned to business goals and strategic OKRs.

• Predictable execution — Stable priorities, visible governance, and consistent delivery against agreed outcomes.

• Stronger lending performance — Improvements in conversion, STP rate, credit performance and unit economics.

• Validated new products — Successful launch and validation of new initiatives meeting defined PMF criteria.

• Stronger team capability — High engagement and measurable lift in product craft.

• Data-led culture — Portfolio decisions grounded in experimentation and measurable outcomes.

Pay.com.au is a high-growth fintech redefining B2B payments, helping over 50,000 businesses process billions in payments and earn billions in points through its PayRewards program. They turn everyday business spend into extraordinary value through transfer partners such as Qantas, Virgin Australia and Singapore Airlines.

Founded in Melbourne, the company sits at the intersection of payments, credit and loyalty. Businesses use the platform to pay suppliers, manage expenses and earn rewards.

The company has grown rapidly in recent years, with revenue more than doubling in FY2025. As part of its next stage of growth, Pay.com.au is expanding internationally and preparing for a potential ASX listing.

The Role

We're looking for an AI Adoption Lead to help identify and implement practical ways AI can improve how the business operates.

This is not a product management role, nor is it a pure engineering position. Instead, the focus is on experimentation, rapid prototyping and applying AI to real operational problems across the organisation.

The goal is to find someone who enjoys building and testing things. Someone who is constantly experimenting with new AI tools, thinking about practical use cases, and building lightweight solutions that make teams more effective.

A large part of the role will involve identifying friction in existing workflows, testing whether AI can improve them, building simple MVPs and iterating based on real feedback from teams.

The emphasis is on speed, experimentation and practical outcomes rather than large transformation programs.

What You’ll Be Doing

Identify opportunities where AI can improve internal workflows across operations, marketing, sales and other teams
Rapidly prototype AI-powered tools, automations or workflows
Test ideas quickly and iterate based on feedback from internal teams
Work with engineers and data teams to safely deploy useful solutions
Experiment with emerging AI tools and frameworks
Help build internal momentum and capability around AI adoption
Translate AI capabilities into practical business applications
This role reports directly to a senior leader in the business and will work closely with engineering, data, marketing and operations teams.
Areas Where AI Could Make an Impact

Customer verification and operational workflows that currently involve manual steps
Marketing asset creation and campaign experimentation
Extracting insights from sales conversations and internal communications
Agent-based workflow automation across internal systems
These areas are starting points rather than a fixed roadmap. The expectation is that the person hired will identify additional opportunities as they go.

Technology Environment

The role will interact with a range of internal systems and data sources, including AWS infrastructure, Databricks and other operational platforms used across the business.

You’ll also work closely with engineers, analysts and data scientists who can help support the development and deployment of useful solutions.

What Success Looks Like

In the first three to six months, success will come from building working prototypes and demonstrating clear value for internal teams. Examples might include automating a repetitive operational task, building a tool to extract insights from internal data, or creating an AI workflow to speed up a marketing or sales process.

Over time, the goal is to embed AI into several core workflows across the business and help teams work more efficiently.

About You

This role will suit someone who spends a lot of time experimenting with AI tools and thinking about how to apply them to real problems.

You don’t need to be a deep machine learning specialist, but you should be comfortable working with modern AI tooling.

You must be someone who:

Regularly experiments with new AI tools and models
Enjoy building prototypes, MVPs and small automation tools
Think about how AI can improve real workflows
Are comfortable working independently in a lean environment
Are confident in challenging existing processes
Have experience working with tools such as Python, LLM APIs, automation platforms or modern AI frameworks
Supporting Information

Hybrid role, 3 days in and 2 at home. Office currently in Elsternwick, soon to relocate to Windsor
Salary: $120-150k + super, negotiable for someone exceptional
Interested?

Apply now or email matt@ponyexpresstalent.com

Job Title: Snr Functional & Technical Analyst D365 SCO
Ad Text:
About us:
Snackbrands Australia is part of Intersnack Group, a private German snack food company founded in 1968. Although internationally owned we are still very much a local company Creating Happy Snacking Moments for everyone!


Here at Snack Brands Australia, we proudly make some of Australia’s favourite snacks including Kettle, CC’s, Thins, Cheezels, The Natural Chip Company and Nice & Natural. Across our three sites, we make over 200 million packets of chips a year - that's a lot of enjoyment and fun we bring to snackers every day! We encourage a culture that is exciting, challenging and we strive for innovation in everything we do. Our aim is for our people to share in our success, enjoy our products and reap the benefits of their hard work within a supportive environment where work life balance is encouraged!


About the role:


We’re looking for an experienced Snr Functional & Technical Analyst D365 SCO to join our IT Applications team and drive the successful delivery of D365 projects, enhancements and support across our Operations function.

This role will be based in Orchard Hills with hybrid working 

What you’ll be responsible for:


Leading the transition from legacy systems to Dynamics 365 F&O Supply Chain modules, performing fit-gap analyses and configuring modules to meet specific business workflows..
Conduct workshops with stakeholders to define business requirements.
Develop critical project artifacts, including Business Requirement Specifications (BRS), Functional Design Documents (FDD), technical requirements and process flows.
Reduce the Production and Logistic reliance on legacy solutions by migrating to Dynamics 365 Operations functionality.
Conduct Dynamics F&O working sessions to demonstrate system capabilities by configuring core modules, data migration and reporting to enable workflows that meet business requirements.
Partner with Finance, Production, and Logistics teams to ensure seamless and optimised end-to-end data flow and reporting.
Support supply chain and manufacturing users including production problems, incidents and change requests i.e. manage and resolve open tickets in your support queue.
Follow ITIL processes and methodologies for approval and migration of changes to production
Collaborate with Operations, Project Office and IT teams to resolve issues across operations, manufacturing, warehousing, distribution and inventory.
Partner with supply chain and operations departments across the business to ensure solutions meet operational requirements and align with business milestones.
Coordinate project plans, budgets and reporting.
Influence best practice solution architecture and support a high-performance IT culture.
Work with vendors to provide support and upgrade information.
Develop, execute and lead unit, system and user testing as required to implement changes into production
Build strong relationships across stakeholders and business users. 

What you’ll bring to the role:
Proven experience as a Senior Functional and Technical analyst in Dynamics F&O Supply Chain modules.
Experience in FMCG processes including order management, demand and supply planning, MRP, manufacturing, warehouse management, inventory management, procurement.
Excellent Dynamics 365 and business process knowledge in Supply Chain modules.
Solution knowledge of Dynamics 365, SQL data base and integration technologies.
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement and influencing skills.
Strong analytical, project planning and commercial acumen.
Ability to manage multiple initiatives, navigate conflicting priorities and work with senior leaders 


What can you expect in return?
A supportive working environment (with plenty of snacks on hand!) where you'll feel engaged and valued, career growth, Birthday Leave, Access to discounts on Retailers, onsite parking (including electric car charging stations) and a focus on health and wellness (like Wellbeing Days!) to make your workplace more enjoyable!



**Our application process includes an Application Form that will be emailed to you directly after your submission. Please ensure you check your junk inbox for this application form, as only applications with this form will be considered. Thank you

The Role

At Trideca, we are looking for a bold, hands-on Chief Technology Officer who is excited by the opportunity to build something truly significant  — your own AI practice from the ground up.


Opportunities like this are rare!!! Your core mandate will be to build and scale Trideca's AI capability, with the autonomy and support to develop, grow, and commercialize AI solutions that deliver meaningful outcomes for both Trideca and our enterprise clients. 

As Trideca's most senior technology leader, this role offers the chance to define the vision, shape the capability, and lead the next phase of growth for both the traditional engineering team and AI-driven transformation. You will play a pivotal role in establishing how AI becomes embedded in our services, our solutions, and the value we deliver to some of Australia's largest organizations.

We are looking for a leader who loves getting into the detail with engineers, architecting world-class solutions, and leading by example. This is a true lead-by-doing role where you will work alongside brilliant technologists, challenge thinking, and ensure everything we deliver is technically exceptional and commercially impactful.

You will be responsible for creating and scaling Trideca's AI capability in addition to the engineering roadmap, where you will define the strategy, build and develop the team, develop the offerings, and take cutting-edge technology solutions to enterprise clients. From shaping our AI roadmap to winning new opportunities and delivering transformative engineering programs, you will sit at the center of how Trideca grows its technology footprint.

We are looking for a practical visionary, someone who sees the possibilities of AI but also knows how to turn ideas into real outcomes, not just another pilot. You're energized by emerging technologies, passionate about best practice, and excited by the challenge of building something meaningful from day one.

You have partnered with enterprise clients, led complex technology initiatives, and know how to connect technology, strategy, and commercial value. You're curious, ambitious, and thrive in environments where you can challenge the norm and push boundaries.

If you have ever wanted the autonomy and backing to build and scale an AI practice inside a respected technology consultancy, this is that opportunity.

How will you make an impact:

Lead and shape Trideca's next phase of growth by setting the strategic direction for how technology and AI are leveraged internally and for our clients.

Establish and scale a greenfield AI practice, defining the vision, capability roadmap, service offerings, and commercial strategy to position Trideca as a leading AI transformation partner.

Build and grow a high-performing team of engineers, consultants, and strategists who are passionate about solving complex problems and delivering exceptional outcomes for clients.

Develop strong executive relationships with key clients, deeply understanding their strategic objectives and aligning Trideca's technology and AI solutions to deliver measurable value.

Drive go-to-market initiatives that connect emerging technologies, AI capabilities, and customer outcomes to generate new business opportunities and expand Trideca's market presence.

Partner closely with the executive leadership team to turn strategic ideas into actionable outcomes, identifying new growth opportunities across technology, AI, and digital transformation.

Design and deliver pragmatic transformation roadmaps that leverage data, automation, and artificial intelligence to solve real business challenges and deliver lasting commercial impact.

Foster a culture of experimentation, excellence, and accountability, encouraging teams to innovate, challenge the status quo, and deliver high-quality solutions at pace. As a leader, you will champion best practice and continuously evolve our processes and delivery methodologies.

Own the financial performance of the technology function, including budget management, forecasting, cost centre accountability, and ensuring sustainable growth and profitability.


About You
﻿You are a builder and strategic leader with a passion for impact. You've built technology practices before, and you know what it takes to blend vision, innovation, and flawless execution.

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Demonstrated experience designing, building, and scaling AI, machine learning, and data-driven solutions within complex technology environments.

Strong understanding of modern AI development frameworks and data platforms with the ability to translate emerging AI capabilities into practical, enterprise-ready solutions.

Hands-on credibility across the full AI lifecycle, from data strategy and model development to deployment, governance, and commercial application.

Proven success building and leading a technology practice from the ground up - from winning and growing new customers, expanding capability, and building teams to managing the P&L. We want to understand the successes, failures, and learnings from your experience.

Deep experience in consulting or professional services, ideally within a complex enterprise environment.

Having a customer-first delivery focus is imperative for this role. Trideca is not a product environment, and alignment to our company value of Customer Comes First is paramount.

Strong commercial acumen. You understand how decisions impact profitability, growth, and client value within your department.

A strong personal network and ability to identify and generate new business opportunities that align with Trideca's strategy the drive for growth both individually and as a business is critical, we all want to win at Trideca!

Exceptional leadership skills. This role doesn't come with an abundance of people at your disposal, we have a small but powerful internal team, and everyone is hands-on and accountable.

Clear thought leadership on AI adoption and business integration You are the expert, the advisor and the go-to person for our future tech landscape at Trideca.

A hands-on, builder mindset - you will be comfortable moving between vision and execution and bringing the team with you.

Have served in a leadership role for more than 5 years.

Have led teams of at least 30 people and scaled teams by 40% per year.
﻿

Professional traits that are necessary for this leader:

Exhibits excellent judgment

Hires great people. Develops great people.

Has relentlessly high standards

Thinks strategically, but stays on top of tactical execution

Thinks big and stands firm in their convictions

Results-oriented

Has the innate ability to inspire passion in others


Join us and shape the next era of digital transformation: www.trideca.com.au

About Us

Praemium is a leading provider of next generation wealth management solutions, empowering advisers to deliver exceptional outcomes for high net worth clients.

The recent acquisition of Technotia Laboratories brings world class scientific, engineering and design expertise in house, significantly strengthening Praemium’s advanced technology, computing and machine intelligence capabilities.

By combining trusted wealth management experience with proprietary innovation, Praemium is setting a new benchmark for automation, precision and scalable advice delivery.

Continuous Improvement. Intelligent innovation. Client-first outcomes. The Praemium way.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Senior Investment Analyst to join our Investment Managers & Governance team. This is a senior, high-impact role responsible for helping ensure Praemium’s Investment Menus remain relevant and compliant in an increasingly complex regulatory environment.

Reporting to the Head of Investment Governance, you will play a critical role in driving fund review, oversight, and investment governance across the platform. You will conduct in-depth due diligence on external investment managers across a broad range of asset classes, provide ongoing investment monitoring, and present recommendations to Investment Committees to support informed decision-making. This role is ideally in Sydney, with a hybrid working model (approximately 2–3 days per week in the office)

This role is suited to a seasoned investment professional looking to step into a senior governance-focused position with meaningful influence over platform investment outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Conduct comprehensive investment and organisational due diligence on external fund managers across multiple asset classes, including single-strategy and multi-manager investment
Produce high-quality research, analysis, and written due diligence reports to support investment recommendations for Platform Investment Menu
Present investment analysis, monitoring outcomes, and recommendations to Investment Committees and other senior stakeholders
Undertake ongoing investment monitoring, including periodic manager reviews, performance and returns analysis, and identification of investments requiring further investigation
Prepare and coordinate attribution analysis in liaison with fund managers
Manage and maintain investment manager data, research databases, and analytical tools
Support onboarding of new investment managers and related operational activities

Desired Qualifications, Experience and Competencies

Degree in Finance or a related discipline
CFA qualification or progress toward CFA accreditation is highly regarded
Minimum 7 years’ experience in multi-manager fund research, asset consulting, and/or fund ratings (essential)
Proven experience researching funds across multiple asset classes
Strong understanding of investment markets, platform financial products, and their use by financial advisers and clients
Experience with managed fund structures and Model / SMA operations (desirable)
Familiarity with retail investment and superannuation regulation, including trustee requirements (highly regarded)
Experience presenting investment recommendations and reviews to Investment Committees and managing complex stakeholder expectations
Outstanding written, verbal, and presentation communication skills
Demonstrated ability to exercise sound investment judgement and influence outcomes through well-reasoned recommendations
Advanced capability in Microsoft Excel; experience with Morningstar Direct and FactSet preferred

What We Offer

Permanent full-time opportunity
Flexible hybrid work environment, up to 2 days from home
Annual bonus opportunity
Access to novated leasing, financial wellness programs and learning opportunities
Tailored professional development
Modern office with end-of-trip facilities at 264 George Street, Sydney

How to Apply

Please apply with your CV, emphasising how your experience and career aligns with the key responsibilities this role. Applicants must have full, unrestricted Australian working rights. Police checks apply to successful candidates.
